title: dactylorhiza sambucina var. laurentina
intro: Dactylorhiza sambucina var. laurentina is a rare and endemic subspecies of orchid found in the Laurentian region of Quebec, Canada. This plant is also known as the 'Laurentian marsh-orchid' and is characterized by its delicate white or pale yellow flowers and long, thin leaves. Dactylorhiza sambucina var. laurentina has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections, wounds, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits and optimal usage.
